User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the Cyber Gladiators application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, matching Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: Role of the user (Teacher or Student).

xp.description: Total experience points earned by the user.

Room.title: Battle Room
Room.description: Represents a battle room where students take a quiz.

teacherId.description: The UID of the teacher who created the room.

quiz.description: A denormalized copy of the entire Quiz object for this battle.

studentIds.description: An array of student UIDs who have joined the room.

status.description: The current status of the battle.

currentQuestionIndex.description: The index of the currently active question.

startTime.description: Timestamp when the battle started playing.

battleResultIds.description: An array of IDs for the BattleResult documents.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the room was created.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents a quiz created by a teacher. This is denormalized into a Room.

teacherId.description: The UID of the teacher who created the quiz.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the quiz was created.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question within a quiz.

correctAnswerIndex.description: Index of the correct answer in the answerOptions array.

timer.description: Timer duration (in seconds) for the question.

BattleResult.title: Battle Result
BattleResult.description: Represents the result of a student's participation in a battle.

structure.description: Stores all battle rooms. Contains denormalized quiz data.

firestore.reasoning: This simplified structure uses top-level collections for all major data types. This makes security rules significantly easier to write and maintain, which was a major source of bugs in the previous design. Denormalizing the 'quiz' object directly into the 'battleRoom' document eliminates the need for extra database reads and complex rules involving `get()` calls, which improves performance and security. This flat structure is robust, scalable, and directly addresses the core requirements of the app in the most straightforward way possible.
